observations: Workspace is a two-crate Cargo workspace with ylong_http and ylong_http_client.
ylong_http_client contains util::proxy, async_impl/connector, sync_impl/connector, HTTPS proxy benchmark binary, README benchmark claims, and proxy tests.
Recent git history includes HTTPS proxy support, tunnel validation hardening, and HTTPS proxy hot-path optimization commits.
parse_tunnel_response obtains the three-digit status code and currently matches b"200" as the only successful tunnel response.
Current unit tests cover 200 success, 407 auth failure, 500 failure, malformed status, and incomplete headers, but do not cover 201 or 204 success.
relevant_lines: parse_tunnel_response match code arm currently treats b"200" as success and b"407" as authentication failure.

observations: Proxy::tls_config stores a TlsConfig on proxy endpoint configuration.
https_proxy_bench reads YLONG_PROXY_CA_FILE, YLONG_PROXY_CERT_FILE, YLONG_PROXY_KEY_FILE, YLONG_PROXY_CIPHER_LIST, and YLONG_PROXY_INSECURE for the proxy TLS hop.
The inspected proxy tests include a unit test that a TlsConfig can be attached to an HTTPS proxy, but no hard endpoint test proving proxy mTLS positive and negative behavior was found in this pass.
README says the benchmark compares ylong_http_client with curl/libcurl and presents 95%+ local improvements.

https_proxy_bench invokes a curl executable from YLONG_CURL and writes repeated URL entries to a curl config file, which is curl CLI/process baseline evidence unless a libcurl library harness is separately implemented.
The Python benchmark driver starts a local HTTPS proxy and uses a local HTTP target URL by default.
https_proxy_bench reads YLONG_CURL as an executable path and invokes it through std::process::Command.
The benchmark writes a curl config file with repeated URL entries and measures the spawned curl process elapsed time.
No same-process libcurl-library benchmark path, libcurl Rust binding, or C libcurl harness is present in the inspected benchmark files.
Current benchmark output parses only total elapsed time for ylong_http_client and curl.
Current benchmark summaries derive latency/request, throughput, and improvement versus curl, but do not collect p50, p95, CPU/request, RSS, confidence intervals, profiling, tracing, or ablation data.

Current benchmark source uses the public ylong_http_client async ClientBuilder and request path for ylong traffic, but no evidence artifact yet proves real-client-path coverage or rules out benchmark-only shortcuts after future benchmark changes.

commands_used: rg -n "https proxy|HTTPS proxy|proxy TLS|mTLS|CONNECT|parse_tunnel_response|Proxy::.*https|tls_config|curl|libcurl|95|Improvement|benchmark" README.md README_zh.md docs/user_guide.md ylong_http_client/src ylong_http_client/tests -g '*.rs' -g '*.md'
